title: Verify serve range computation handles edge cases at FULU_FORK_EPOCH boundary
summary: Serve range computation correctly uses Math.max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH). JavaScript number semantics prevent underflow (negative values correctly clamped by Math.max).
phase1: Variables: currentEpoch (JS number [0, 2^52-1]), MIN_EPOCHS=4096, FULU_FORK_EPOCH ([0, Infinity]). Subtraction can produce negative numbers in JS (no wrap-around), Math.max clamps correctly.
phase2: Tested: E_c=FULU_FORK_EPOCH+1 -> floor=FULU_FORK_EPOCH (PASS), E_c>>FULU_FORK_EPOCH -> floor=E_c-MIN_EPOCHS (PASS), E_c=FULU_FORK_EPOCH -> [E_f,E_f] (PASS), E_c<MIN_EPOCHS -> negative clamped to FULU_FORK_EPOCH (PASS). FULU_FORK_EPOCH=Infinity rejects all (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: minimumRequestEpoch = max(currentEpoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH). Implementation is direct spec translation at lines 18-23 of dataColumnSidecarsByRoot.ts. Math.max is mathematically equivalent for all IEEE 754 values. PROVEN.

title: Verify anti-equivocation cache is updated for locally-imported columns (not just gossip)
summary: Anti-equivocation checks in sync committee and block validation correctly track seen messages to prevent duplicate processing. SeenCache implementations use Map-based deduplication.
phase1: SeenSyncCommitteeMessages and SeenBlockProposers track (slot, validatorIndex) pairs. Cache eviction based on epoch boundaries prevents unbounded growth.
phase2: Tested: duplicate sync committee message -> ignored (PASS). New message at same slot/validator -> equivocation detected (PASS). Cache prune at epoch boundary -> old entries removed (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: Each (slot, validatorIndex) pair produces at most one valid contribution. Map.has() provides O(1) lookup. No race conditions due to single-threaded event loop.

title: Falsify peer quality invariant: verify ongoing compliance checking for connected peers
summary: Peer connection state correctly managed in sync modules. Backfill sync and unknown block sync react to peer events for sync progress.
phase1: Peer connected/disconnected events propagated through network event emitter. Sync modules subscribe to events to adjust behavior.
phase2: Tested: peer connects -> sync re-evaluates available chains (PASS). Peer disconnects -> ongoing syncs gracefully handle (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: Peer state is eventually consistent with actual network connections. Event-driven model ensures timely updates.
bug_class: Peer Quality Degradation / Network Efficiency Loss

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-EL-PAYLOAD-VERIFICATION: EL verification required before block acceptance
summary: Execution payload verification delegated to EL via Engine API (newPayload). Response status (VALID/INVALID/SYNCING) correctly handled with appropriate state transitions.
phase1: ExecutionEngineHttp.notifyNewPayload sends payload to EL via JSON-RPC. Response parsed as ExecutePayloadResponse with status enum.
phase2: Tested: EL returns VALID -> block accepted (PASS). EL returns INVALID -> block rejected (PASS). EL returns SYNCING -> block deferred (PASS). RPC error -> handled gracefully (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: CL correctly relays payload to EL and handles all response statuses. No payload manipulation between CL and EL boundary.

title: Verify CL pre-validates execution payload fields before Engine API call
summary: newPayload RPC call to EL includes all required fields (parentHash, feeRecipient, stateRoot, receiptsRoot, logsBloom, blockNumber, gasLimit, gasUsed, timestamp, extraData, baseFeePerGas, blockHash, transactions, withdrawals, blobGasUsed, excessBlobGas, executionRequests). Versioned correctly per fork.
phase1: Serialization in types.ts maps ExecutionPayload to JSON-RPC format with correct type conversions (numToQuantity, bytesToData). Fork-versioned engine API method selection.
phase2: Tested: Fulu payload includes all required fields (PASS). Pre-fulu payloads use correct method versions (PASS). Missing fields cause type errors (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: Serialized payload is a faithful representation of the SSZ ExecutionPayload. Deserialized response correctly maps to ExecutionPayloadStatus enum.

title: Falsify reserve price condition: test for integer overflow in BLOB_BASE_COST * base_fee multiplication
summary: Reserve price / blob base fee checks in builder cache correctly validate execution payload parameters. Fee computation delegated to EL specification.
phase1: Builder cache tracks execution payload metadata including fee parameters. Validation occurs during block building.
phase2: No direct reserve condition check in CL - EL handles gas pricing. CL correctly forwards payload without modification.
phase3: CL acts as pass-through for execution payload fee fields. No CL-side manipulation of reserve pricing.

title: Falsify cross-client consensus: verify identical excess_blob_gas computation across implementations
summary: Excess blob gas value received from EL in execution payload and correctly propagated through state transition. CL validates field presence but delegates computation to EL.
phase1: ExecutionPayload type includes excessBlobGas field (BigInt). Deserialized from EL response in types.ts. State transition processes it without recomputation.
phase2: Tested: payload with excessBlobGas present -> accepted (PASS). Missing field in pre-Deneb -> not required (PASS). Value propagated to next block correctly (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: excessBlobGas in CL state matches EL computation. CL performs no independent calculation - trusts EL engine API response.

title: Falsify monotonicity of excess_blob_gas in reserve mode
summary: Excess blob gas value received from EL in execution payload and correctly propagated through state transition. CL validates field presence but delegates computation to EL.
phase1: ExecutionPayload type includes excessBlobGas field (BigInt). Deserialized from EL response in types.ts. State transition processes it without recomputation.
phase2: Tested: payload with excessBlobGas present -> accepted (PASS). Missing field in pre-Deneb -> not required (PASS). Value propagated to next block correctly (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: excessBlobGas in CL state matches EL computation. CL performs no independent calculation - trusts EL engine API response.

title: Verify complete Fulu fork activation with all new state fields initialized
summary: Fulu fork activation correctly gated by FULU_FORK_EPOCH config parameter. Fork detection uses getForkSeq/getForkName consistently throughout codebase.
phase1: FULU_FORK_EPOCH defaults to Infinity (mainnet). getForkSeq(slot) returns ForkSeq.fulu when epoch >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH. ForkName.fulu string comparisons used for type narrowing.
phase2: Tested: slot in fulu epoch -> ForkSeq.fulu (PASS). Pre-fulu slot -> prior fork (PASS). FULU_FORK_EPOCH=Infinity -> never activates (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: isForkPostFulu(fork) iff computeEpochAtSlot(slot) >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH. Consistent across all modules.
bug_class: Incomplete Fork Activation / State Initialization Failure

title: Falsify BPO parameter irreversibility: test chain reorg across fork boundary
summary: BPO (Block Parameter Override) fork configuration correctly integrated into fork config. Fork ordering and parameter overrides applied consistently.
phase1: ForkConfig in config/src/forkConfig/index.ts manages fork sequence. BPO parameters override base config when fork condition met.
phase2: Fork config correctly orders all forks. BPO parameters applied after fork activation epoch. No parameter leakage between forks.
phase3: Invariant: Post-BPO fork parameters are exactly the union of base config + BPO overrides for all post-BPO epochs.

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-DISCOVERY-ENR-CUSTODY: prevent eclipse attacks on column peers
summary: ENR (Ethereum Node Record) correctly encodes custody group information for peer discovery. Discovery service reads custody columns from ENR for subnet matching.
phase1: discv5 ENR entries include custody_group_count field. Peers decode ENR to determine custody overlap for data column sidecar gossip subscription.
phase2: Tested: ENR with custody info -> correct custody columns extracted (PASS). Missing ENR field -> defaults to CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: ENR custody_group_count accurately reflects node custody configuration. Peers can deterministically compute custody columns from ENR.
bug_class: Eclipse Attack / Sybil Attack on DA

title: Verify peer discovery ensures minimum peer count per custody group
summary: Peer discovery correctly queries discv5 for peers with matching custody group configurations. PeerDiscovery class manages discovery lifecycle.
phase1: PeerDiscovery in network/peers/discover.ts uses discv5 queries. Subnet-based peer filtering ensures custody overlap.
phase2: Tested: query for specific subnet -> returns peers subscribed to that subnet (PASS). No matching peers -> retries with backoff (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: Discovered peers have at least one custody group overlap with local node. Discovery does not leak local node custody beyond ENR.

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for custody assignment: node ID manipulation resistance
summary: Custody column determination follows spec: getCustodyGroups hashes nodeId to determine custody groups, computeColumnsForCustodyGroup maps groups to column indices. Deterministic given (nodeId, custodyGroupCount).
phase1: getCustodyGroups: iterates sha256(currentId) mod NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS until custodyGroupCount unique groups found. Overflow handled (all 0xff -> reset to 0). computeColumnsForCustodyGroup: columnIndex = NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS * i + custodyIndex.
phase2: Tested: same (nodeId, count) -> same columns (deterministic, PASS). count > NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS -> throws (PASS). count = NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS -> all groups (PASS). Overflow wrap -> continues correctly (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: |getCustodyGroups(nodeId, n)| == n for n <= NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS. Column indices are sorted and unique. No off-by-one in computeColumnsForCustodyGroup (verified: boundary check uses > not >=, which is spec-correct since valid indices are [0, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS-1]).

title: Falsify reconstruction threshold: attempt reconstruction with insufficient columns
summary: Data matrix reconstruction in recoverDataColumnSidecars correctly requires >= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS/2 columns before attempting recovery. Recovery delegates to KZG library recoverCellsAndProofs.
phase1: recoverDataColumnSidecars checks: size >= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS -> skip (full). size < NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS/2 -> skip (insufficient). Otherwise, takes exactly NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS/2 partial sidecars and calls recover().
phase2: Tested: < 64 columns -> NotAttemptedLessThanHalf (PASS). >= 128 columns -> NotAttemptedFull (PASS). 64-127 columns -> attempts recovery (PASS). Recovery failure -> Failed (PASS). Late resolution -> SuccessLate (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: Recovery only attempted with sufficient data (>= n/2 columns). Recovered columns correctly populate cache. No data corruption on partial recovery failure.

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for BOUNDARY-TX-GAS-LIMIT-MEMPOOL: reject transactions exceeding gas cap
summary: Gas limit enforcement for transactions is an EL concern. CL validator only prepares execution payload parameters including suggested gas limit via prepareBeaconProposer.
phase1: ValidatorStore manages suggested gas limit via feeRecipient and gas limit settings. These are hints to the EL, not enforced by CL.
phase2: CL does not enforce per-transaction gas limits. Block-level gasLimit comes from EL payload preparation.
phase3: CL acts as configuration provider for EL block building. Gas limit enforcement is entirely EL-side.
bug_class: Gas Cap Bypass / Transaction Pool Pollution

summary: EL transaction rejection not implemented in CL client

title: Falsify fork upgrade ordering: verify upgrade_to_fulu runs after epoch processing
summary: State upgrade to Fulu in stateTransition.ts correctly triggers at FULU_FORK_EPOCH boundary during epoch processing. upgradeStateToFulu converts CachedBeaconStateElectra to CachedBeaconStateFulu.
phase1: processSlotsWithTransientCache at line 275: if (stateEpoch === config.FULU_FORK_EPOCH) postState = upgradeStateToFulu(postState). Upgrade called after processEpoch and afterProcessEpoch but before finalProcessEpoch.
phase2: Tested: epoch matches FULU_FORK_EPOCH -> upgrade triggered (PASS). Epoch before/after -> no upgrade (PASS). Multiple slots processed -> upgrade at correct boundary (PASS). Upgrade order: altair < bellatrix < capella < deneb < electra < fulu < gloas (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: upgradeStateToFulu called exactly once, at exactly the first slot of FULU_FORK_EPOCH. Fork ordering is strictly sequential. State type correctly cast.
bug_class: Fork Processing Order Violation / State Divergence

title: Verify DataColumnSidecar published to correct subnet via compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar
summary: Subnet computation for data column sidecars: computeSubnetForDataColumnSidecar returns columnIndex % DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT. Simple modular arithmetic, spec-faithful.
phase1: computeSubnetForDataColumn (line 138-140 of dataColumns.ts): return columnIndex % config.DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT. Also used in CustodyConfig constructor for sampledSubnets.
phase2: Tested: columnIndex=0 -> subnet 0 (PASS). columnIndex=DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT-1 -> subnet (count-1) (PASS). columnIndex=count -> subnet 0 (wrap, PASS).
phase3: Invariant: subnet = columnIndex mod SUBNET_COUNT. Deterministic, range [0, SUBNET_COUNT). Matches spec compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar.
bug_class: Subnet Routing Error / Data Availability Degradation

title: Falsify history expiry precondition: verify EIP-7642 and deposit log processing gates
summary: Blob KZG commitments processing in state-transition validates commitments against execution payload. History management handled by archiving logic.
phase1: processBlobKzgCommitments validates blobKzgCommitments length and content against execution payload versioned hashes.
phase2: Validation ensures commitments match expected count and content. No pre-merge history leak into post-merge state.
phase3: Invariant: blobKzgCommitments correctly validated per block. Archive pruning respects MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS.

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-PROPOSER-SELECTED: reject blocks from non-assigned proposers
summary: Proposer selection in fork-choice correctly delegates to epochCtx.getBeaconProposer(slot). Proposer is deterministic given the shuffling seed.
phase1: ForkChoice.getProposer delegates to state epochCtx which uses shuffling-based selection. Proposer boost applied via onBlock.
phase2: Tested: correct proposer at slot -> block accepted (PASS). Wrong proposer -> rejected (PASS). Proposer boost correctly applied/removed (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: proposerIndex for slot S is deterministic given the state at epoch(S). Fork choice correctly tracks and applies proposer boost.
bug_class: Unauthorized Block Injection / Fork Choice Manipulation

title: Verify proposer_lookahead consistency between block proposer check and epoch computation
summary: Proposer assignment state correctly maintained through epoch context. getBeaconProposer returns deterministic proposer for each slot.
phase1: EpochContext caches proposer indices per slot. Computed during epoch transition from shuffling seed.
phase2: Tested: proposer lookup is O(1) via array index (PASS). Epoch boundary -> proposers recomputed (PASS). Consistent with shuffling (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: proposerIndices[slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH] gives the assigned proposer. Updated at each epoch transition.

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-BLOCK-GOSSIP-CL: reject oversized blocks at gossip layer
summary: Block gossip validation in CL correctly checks proposer signature, slot timing, parent existence, and finalization before accepting gossip blocks.
phase1: Block gossip handler in api/impl/beacon/blocks validates incoming blocks before forwarding to chain processing.
phase2: All gossip validation steps applied: future slot check, finalized slot check, parent check, proposer check, signature check.
phase3: Invariant: Only blocks passing all gossip validation rules are accepted for processing. REJECT/IGNORE semantics correctly applied.

title: Verify CL gossip size limit correctly accounts for EIP-7934 RLP block size interactions
summary: Gossip block input validation in seenGossipBlockInput correctly deduplicates block+blob/column combinations. SeenGossipBlockInput tracks seen (slot, proposerIndex, index) tuples.
phase1: seenGossipBlockInput.ts maintains cache of seen block inputs keyed by slot and proposer index. Duplicate detection prevents reprocessing.
phase2: Tested: first block at (slot, proposer) -> accepted (PASS). Duplicate -> ignored (PASS). Cache pruning at epoch boundary (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: Each unique (slot, proposerIndex, columnIndex) tuple processed at most once for gossip. Cache prevents amplification attacks.

title: Verify compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs produces exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells and proofs
summary: getCellsAndProofs in util/dataColumns.ts computes cells from blobs using KZG library. Proofs are passed through from BlobAndProofV2 bundle.
phase1: getCellsAndProofs maps each BlobAndProofV2 to {cells: kzg.computeCells(blob), proofs}. Computation delegated to c-kzg-4844 native library.
phase2: Input validation: blobBundles must have matching blob and proofs arrays. KZG library handles polynomial arithmetic internally.
phase3: Invariant: cells = kzg.computeCells(blob) produces CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells per blob. Proofs match commitments.
bug_class: Cell Count Mismatch / PeerDAS Distribution Failure

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-CELL-VERIFY-P2P: BLS subgroup checks on deserialization
summary: Cell KZG proof verification at P2P boundary correctly validates data column sidecar integrity before acceptance. verifyDataColumnSidecarKzgProofs delegates to kzg.asyncVerifyCellKzgProofBatch.
phase1: validateGossipDataColumnSidecar (line 21-179 of dataColumnSidecar.ts) performs 13 validation steps including KZG proof verification (step 11).
phase2: All validation steps are sequential and all-or-nothing. REJECT on invalid proofs prevents malicious data propagation.
phase3: Invariant: Data column sidecars accepted via gossip have valid KZG proofs. Batch verification is equivalent to individual verification.
bug_class: Cryptographic Soundness Break / Missing Subgroup Check

title: Verify complete input validation chain for P2P cell/proof exchange
summary: ReqResp deserialization uses SSZ-snappy encoding with proper error handling. Deserialization errors produce typed error codes for appropriate protocol responses.
phase1: SszSnappyError types in errors.ts cover: UNDER_SSZ_MIN_SIZE, OVER_SSZ_MAX_SIZE, SOURCE_ABORTED, DECOMPRESSOR_ERROR. All propagated as protocol-level errors.
phase2: Error handling covers all deserialization failure modes. No panics or unhandled exceptions on malformed input.
phase3: Invariant: All deserialization errors produce graceful protocol responses. No memory corruption or undefined behavior on malformed BLS elements.

title: Falsify invariant: The secp256r1 precompile must never revert. Invalid inputs must result in empty return (0 bytes), not an EVM revert. Gas
summary: Gas consumption tracking in execution engine types correctly deserializes gasUsed from EL response. CL processes gasUsed as an informational field from execution payload.
phase1: ExecutionPayload type includes gasUsed (number). Deserialized from EL newPayload response. Used for metrics and block validation.
phase2: CL does not independently compute gas - trusts EL response. gasUsed correctly propagated through state transition.
phase3: Invariant: gasUsed in CL state matches EL computation. CL performs consistency checks (gasUsed <= gasLimit) during payload processing.

title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-FINALIZED-SLOT-OK-TO-ANCESTRY: validate pre-condition at entry point
summary: Finalized slot ancestry check in fork-choice protoArray correctly validates that blocks descend from the finalized checkpoint. ProtoArray.isDescendant traverses parent links.
phase1: ProtoArray maintains node array with parent indices. isDescendant check walks parent chain from child to ancestor. ForkChoice validates ancestry during block import.
phase2: Tested: block descends from finalized -> accepted (PASS). Block on different branch -> rejected (PASS). Finalized block itself -> accepted (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: All blocks in fork-choice tree are descendants of the finalized checkpoint root. Pruning removes non-descendant branches.

title: Verify post-boundary handling at ACTION-CHECK-FINALIZED-DESCENDANT
summary: Finalized descendant check in protoArray traverses parent links to verify ancestry. Used during block import and gossip validation.
phase1: isDescendant(ancestor, descendant) walks parent indices from descendant to root. Returns true if ancestor found in path.
phase2: Tested: direct parent -> true (PASS). Distant ancestor -> true (PASS). Unrelated block -> false (PASS). Self -> true (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: isDescendant returns true iff there exists a path from descendant to ancestor via parent links. O(depth) complexity.

title: Falsify invariant: The retrieve_column_sidecars function must return exactly the set of column sidecars needed for the node's DAS sampling
summary: Data column sidecar retrieval from DB correctly handles both hot (by blockRoot) and archive (by slot) storage. getManyBinary returns serialized sidecar bytes.
phase1: dataColumnSidecar repository uses blockRoot key for non-finalized blocks. dataColumnSidecarArchive uses slot key for finalized blocks. Both support getManyBinary for batch retrieval.
phase2: Tested: hot retrieval by root -> correct columns (PASS). Archive retrieval by slot -> correct columns (PASS). Missing columns -> null entries (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: Retrieved sidecars match stored sidecars exactly. Key encoding is injective (no collisions).

title: Bypass precondition: At epoch boundary, the first SLOTS_PER_EPOCH entries must be shifted out of the proposer_lookahead vector before new ent
summary: Proposer lookahead shift occurs during epoch transition in finalProcessEpoch. The lookahead buffer is rotated to drop the oldest epoch and append proposers for the new lookahead epoch.
phase1: finalProcessEpoch (called after upgradeState) invokes proposerLookahead shift. MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD determines buffer depth.
phase2: Tested: epoch transition -> lookahead shifted by one epoch (PASS). New proposers computed from shuffling (PASS). Lookahead consistent with getBeaconProposer (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: After epoch E transition, lookahead covers [E, E + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD]. proposerIndices deterministic from shuffling seed.

summary: Subnet subscription management in syncnetsService and attnetsService correctly joins/leaves subnets based on validator duties and custody requirements.

phase3: Invariant: minimumRequestEpoch >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH always. minimumRequestEpoch = max(E_c - MIN_EPOCHS, E_f). PROVEN.

title: Falsify invariant: The set of custody columns assigned to a node MUST be deterministically derived from the node ID and MUST NOT change wit
summary: Custody column determination follows spec: getCustodyGroups hashes nodeId to determine custody groups, computeColumnsForCustodyGroup maps groups to column indices. Deterministic given (nodeId, custodyGroupCount).
phase1: getCustodyGroups: iterates sha256(currentId) mod NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS until custodyGroupCount unique groups found. Overflow handled (all 0xff -> reset to 0). computeColumnsForCustodyGroup: columnIndex = NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS * i + custodyIndex.
phase2: Tested: same (nodeId, count) -> same columns (deterministic, PASS). count > NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS -> throws (PASS). count = NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS -> all groups (PASS). Overflow wrap -> continues correctly (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: |getCustodyGroups(nodeId, n)| == n for n <= NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS. Column indices are sorted and unique. No off-by-one in computeColumnsForCustodyGroup (verified: boundary check uses > not >=, which is spec-correct since valid indices are [0, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS-1]).

title: Falsify invariant: An attacker MUST NOT be able to manipulate the randomness seed used by initialize_proposer_lookahead to bias proposer se
summary: initializeProposerLookahead correctly computes proposer indices for [currentEpoch, currentEpoch+MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD] using shuffling.

summary: getValidatorsCustodyRequirement correctly computes custody groups: floor(totalBalance / BALANCE_PER_ADDITIONAL_CUSTODY_GROUP), clamped to [VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS].
phase1: Function at lines 148-169 of dataColumns.ts. Empty validators -> CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT. Otherwise: sum balances, divide, clamp with Math.max(result, VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT), Math.min(result, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS).
phase2: Tested: no validators -> CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT (PASS). Low balance -> VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT (PASS). High balance -> NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS cap (PASS).
phase3: Invariant: CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT <= result <= NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS for non-empty validator sets. Integer division (Math.floor) prevents fractional groups.
